title: Job Vacancies at Efezi Southsea Nigeria Limited
content:
Efezi Southsea Nigeria Limited is a hospitality consultant company incorporated in 2009 primarily to provide hospitality services to small and medium hotels.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Hotel Receptionist

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos

Key Roles


	A Hotel Receptionist serves as the first point of contact for guests and is responsible for creating a positive first impression while ensuring smooth front office operations.


Duties and Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Welcome guests warmly and professionally upon arrival.
	Handle guest check-ins and check-outs efficiently.
	Receive and manage room reservations and bookings.
	Answer phone calls and respond to guest inquiries.
	Provide information about hotel facilities, services, and local attractions.
	Handle guest complaints and requests promptly and courteously.
	Process payments and issue receipts.
	Maintain accurate guest records and reservation details.
	Coordinate with Housekeeping and other departments regarding room status.
	Monitor room availability and occupancy levels.
	Ensure the reception area is clean, organized, and presentable.
	Prepare daily reports and handover notes for incoming shifts.
	Assist in promoting hotel services and special offers.


Application Closing Date: 15th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Hotel General Manager

Job Location: Sapele, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Roles


	The General Manager (GM) is responsible for the overall operation, profitability, and success of the hotel. The GM oversees all departments and ensures excellent guest service, efficient operations, and achievement of business goals.


Duties and Responsibilities


	Provide overall leadership and direction for the hotel.
	Develop and implement business strategies to increase revenue and profitability.
	Oversee all hotel departments, including Front Office, Housekeeping, Food & Beverage, Maintenance, Security, and Accounts.
	Ensure excellent guest satisfaction and service standards.
	Prepare and manage budgets, financial reports, and cost-control measures.
	Recruit, train, supervise, and evaluate staff performance.
	Ensure compliance with hotel policies, health and safety regulations, and government requirements.
	Handle major guest complaints and resolve operational issues promptly.
	Monitor occupancy levels, room revenue, and sales performance.
	Build relationships with clients, corporate organizations, travel agencies, and other stakeholders.
	Conduct regular management meetings and departmental reviews.
	Ensure proper maintenance of hotel facilities and assets.
	Drive marketing, sales, and promotional activities to increase business.
	Prepare operational and performance reports for management or owners.


Required Skills:
Leadership Skills:


	Team leadership and supervision
	Decision-making ability
	Conflict resolution
	Staff motivation and development
	Delegation and accountability
	Management Skills.


Salary
N300,000 / Month. 

Application Closing Date: 20th June, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

3.) Hotel Accountant 

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos

Description


	A Hotel Accountant is responsible for managing the financial records, transactions, and reporting activities of a hotel to ensure financial accuracy and profitability.


Key Roles and Responsibilities
Financial Record Keeping:


	Maintain accurate financial records and accounting books.
	Record daily income and expenses.


Revenue Monitoring:


	Reconcile daily sales from rooms, restaurants, bars, and other outlets.
	Verify revenue reports and investigate discrepancies.


Accounts Payable & Receivable:


	Process supplier invoices and payments.
	Monitor outstanding debts and guest accounts.


Payroll Administration:


	Prepare and process staff salaries and wages.
	Ensure statutory deductions are correctly applied.


Budgeting and Cost Control:


	Assist in preparing annual budgets.
	Monitor departmental expenditures and control costs.


Financial Reporting:


	Prepare daily, weekly, monthly, and annual financial reports.
	Generate profit and loss statements, balance sheets, and cash flow reports.


Bank Reconciliation:


	Reconcile bank statements with hotel records.
	Investigate and resolve any discrepancies.


Tax Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with tax regulations.
	Prepare and submit relevant tax documents.


Inventory Control:


	Monitor stock records and conduct inventory audits.
	Work closely with storekeepers and department heads.


Internal Audit Support:


	Ensure financial procedures are followed.
	Assist during internal and external audits.


Skills Required for a Hotel Accountant:
Technical Skills:


	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and practices.
	Proficiency in accounting software and hotel management systems.
	Microsoft Excel and financial reporting skills.
	Budget preparation and financial analysis.
	Tax and statutory compliance knowledge.
	Cash management and bank reconciliation.


Application Closing Date: 15th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control Supervisor",
  "description": "Schlumberger Limited is the world&#39;s leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry&#39;s most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Supervisor

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Description


	The Quality Control Supervisor is responsible for ensuring equipment testing and post-maintenance quality control are performed prior to green tagging or releasing equipment to the Asset Coordinator.
	The Quality Control Supervisor manages daily operation and maintenance of the test facility and manages test facility personnel.
	The Quality Control Supervisor is also responsible for ensuring the Quality Management System and required processes (plant procedures, quality plans, etc) are established, implemented and maintained in accordance with API, ISO, ASTM, etc.


Responsibilities


	Manage the test and quality control (QC) organization.
	Optimize and standardize test and QC processes, procedures and capabilities.
	Manage the tracking and calibration of inspection, measurement and test equipment as well as maintenance of the test facility equipment.
	Periodic technical audits and CAT compliance assessments, while ensuring maintenance related remedial action plan closures.
	Perform technical audits of vendors and suppliers to ensure compliance with and qualification to the Quality Management System
	Develop a continuous improvement culture, initiatives and improvement plans in the assigned location or GeoUnit to improve asset utilization, reliability and cost of service delivery (CoSD).
	Participate in failure investigations as assigned by the GeoUnit Reliability and CoSD or Maintenance Manager and with other functions, ensure that root causes of defects are addressed systematically and expediently.
	Provide quality data books and Certifications of Conformity (COC) to products and services delivered to the customers.


Experience &amp; Exposures


	HND / B.Sc in Engineering &ndash; Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, Petroleum, Materials/Metallurgy.
	Minimum of 7 years experience (inclusive of 2 years in shop operations) in oilfield Completions or other oilfield disciplines, including leadership/supervisory roles held.
	Hands-on competency with Completions technologies for Sand Management Services, Intelligent Completions, and Liner Hangers is an added advantage
	Experience with Maintenance Processes, Workflows and Quality Control
	Knowledge of tubulars, material types, connection thread types, termination fittings, and test fixtures.
	Knowledge of CMMS and Maintenance Methodologies used in the oilfield.
	Knowledge of oilfield QHSE standards and guidelines.
	Strong sense of continuous improvement with relevant certifications and executed projects.

Quality Control Supervisor Job at Schlumberger Limited




        
    

        
        Schlumberger Limited is the world's leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry's most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Supervisor

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Quality Control Supervisor is responsible for ensuring equipment testing and post-maintenance quality control are performed prior to green tagging or releasing equipment to the Asset Coordinator.
	The Quality Control Supervisor manages daily operation and maintenance of the test facility and manages test facility personnel.
	The Quality Control Supervisor is also responsible for ensuring the Quality Management System and required processes (plant procedures, quality plans, etc) are established, implemented and maintained in accordance with API, ISO, ASTM, etc.


Responsibilities


	Manage the test and quality control (QC) organization.
	Optimize and standardize test and QC processes, procedures and capabilities.
	Manage the tracking and calibration of inspection, measurement and test equipment as well as maintenance of the test facility equipment.
	Periodic technical audits and CAT compliance assessments, while ensuring maintenance related remedial action plan closures.
	Perform technical audits of vendors and suppliers to ensure compliance with and qualification to the Quality Management System
	Develop a continuous improvement culture, initiatives and improvement plans in the assigned location or GeoUnit to improve asset utilization, reliability and cost of service delivery (CoSD).
	Participate in failure investigations as assigned by the GeoUnit Reliability and CoSD or Maintenance Manager and with other functions, ensure that root causes of defects are addressed systematically and expediently.
	Provide quality data books and Certifications of Conformity (COC) to products and services delivered to the customers.


Experience & Exposures


	HND / B.Sc in Engineering – Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, Petroleum, Materials/Metallurgy.
	Minimum of 7 years experience (inclusive of 2 years in shop operations) in oilfield Completions or other oilfield disciplines, including leadership/supervisory roles held.
	Hands-on competency with Completions technologies for Sand Management Services, Intelligent Completions, and Liner Hangers is an added advantage
	Experience with Maintenance Processes, Workflows and Quality Control
	Knowledge of tubulars, material types, connection thread types, termination fittings, and test fixtures.
	Knowledge of CMMS and Maintenance Methodologies used in the oilfield.
	Knowledge of oilfield QHSE standards and guidelines.
	Strong sense of continuous improvement with relevant certifications and executed projects.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

title: Job Openings at Critters Veterinary Centre
content:
Critters Veterinary Centre  is a foremost veterinary centre of experienced professionals who provide diagnosis, treatement, x-ray, grooming and boarding services.

We continually improve our system in order to provide exceptional services to our Pawrents who entrust the health and care of their pets to us.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Professional Driver

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking for a reliable, trusthoworthy and safety conscious experienced driver who will provide seamless transportation of goods and employees.


Job Responsibilities


	Provide reliable pick - up and drop - off services for operational purposes.
	Peridoically clean and maintain the vehicle(s) as appropriate.
	Perform periodic inspections of the vehicle and report any mechanical issues to the facility manager for repairs and maintenance.
	Keep up to date alltransportation permits and licenses.
	Abide by all road and safety regulations.


Job Requirements


	3 - 5 years of experience as an experienced driver in a company.
	Valid means of ID and driver's license with a verifiable work history.
	Strong Lagos roads navigation and regulation knowledge.
	Good social skills, responsible work ethic and punctual.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Click here to apply online

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.

 

2.) Client Relationship Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The ideal candidate will manage all customer's requests for services and products offered in the organization, enlighten clients on features and benefits of goods or services to be purchased, and deliver excellent customer experience.


Job Description


	Respond to customer inquiries in a prompt and courteous manner.
	Provide relevant benefits and features of products and services offered.
	Documenting accurately all customer information on organization software.
	Process orders efficiently and accurately.
	Providing timely and consistent feedback to customers.
	Soliciting and resolving customer complaints within the defined resolution timeframe.
	Upselling and cross-selling to clients on products and services.
	Maintain a clean ambiance of the working station and environment.


Job Requirements


	Minimum of BSc / HND qualification in any field of study.
	0-2 years of experience as a customer support or customer service officer.
	Excellent communication, reporting, and interpersonal skills.
	Active listening skills.
	Must be comfortable with pets (dogs & cats).
	Proximity to Lekki is an advantage.


Soft Skills Requirements:


	Emotional Intelligence.
	Team player.


Salary
N250,000 / month.

Click here to apply online

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks&rsquo; segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Abuja

Job Purpose


	The Administrative Officer is responsible for coordinating and overseeing the day-to-day administrative operations of the organization to ensure efficiency, effectiveness, and smooth business functioning.
	You will manage office administration, facility management, vendor coordination, asset management, travel and logistics arrangements, record keeping, and administrative support services.
	Also ensure that organizational resources are effectively utilized, administrative policies are implemented.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage time and attendance biometric capturing, update weekly roaster and generate report for weekly wages payment.
	Manage and process weekly and monthly payments for police and SPU officers
	Ensure regular staff enlightenment on quality management system, while ensuring compliance with quality policy.
	Manage the process of generating staff ID and access cards.
	Work closely with vendors to ensure that the company&rsquo;s fleet are in good working condition.
	Ensure all documentation, licenses, and registrations pertaining to the company&rsquo;s fleet are updated as at when due.
	Process logistic support requests for personnel across the company in relation to travelling on local business (official) trips.
	Manage vendors and ensure SLAs are met, risk is mitigated, and increased value is gained from all vendors.
	Classify, record, and file documents for use in maintaining administrative records for the HR &amp; Admin department.


Qualifications


	First Degree or its equivalent in Business Administration or any related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of job-relevant experience in a well-structured organization, preferably manufacturing or FMCG.
	Professional certification in HR Management e.g. Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM), etc, is required.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Time and Attendance Software Management Skills
	Vendor/Contractor Management Skills
	Admin-related Expenses Management Skills
	SPU Officers Management Skills
	Training Administration Skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: talents@ritefoodsltd.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Equal Opportunity Statement: We are an equal opportunity provider. We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate on the basis of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, disability. We promote a culture of fairness and equal access to opportunities for all qualified applicants.

Administrative Officer Job at Rite Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks’ segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are recruiting to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Abuja

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Administrative Officer is responsible for coordinating and overseeing the day-to-day administrative operations of the organization to ensure efficiency, effectiveness, and smooth business functioning.
	You will manage office administration, facility management, vendor coordination, asset management, travel and logistics arrangements, record keeping, and administrative support services.
	Also ensure that organizational resources are effectively utilized, administrative policies are implemented.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage time and attendance biometric capturing, update weekly roaster and generate report for weekly wages payment.
	Manage and process weekly and monthly payments for police and SPU officers
	Ensure regular staff enlightenment on quality management system, while ensuring compliance with quality policy.
	Manage the process of generating staff ID and access cards.
	Work closely with vendors to ensure that the company’s fleet are in good working condition.
	Ensure all documentation, licenses, and registrations pertaining to the company’s fleet are updated as at when due.
	Process logistic support requests for personnel across the company in relation to travelling on local business (official) trips.
	Manage vendors and ensure SLAs are met, risk is mitigated, and increased value is gained from all vendors.
	Classify, record, and file documents for use in maintaining administrative records for the HR & Admin department.


Qualifications


	First Degree or its equivalent in Business Administration or any related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of job-relevant experience in a well-structured organization, preferably manufacturing or FMCG.
	Professional certification in HR Management e.g. Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM), etc, is required.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Time and Attendance Software Management Skills
	Vendor/Contractor Management Skills
	Admin-related Expenses Management Skills
	SPU Officers Management Skills
	Training Administration Skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Equal Opportunity Statement: We are an equal opportunity provider. We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate on the basis of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, disability. We promote a culture of fairness and equal access to opportunities for all qualified applicants.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Fleet Officer",
  "description": "Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks&rsquo; segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Locations: Port Harcourt - Rivers, and Benin - Edo

Job Purpose


	The Fleet Officer is responsible for managing and coordinating the organization&#39;s fleet operations to ensure the safe, efficient, and cost-effective utilization of vehicles.
	You will be overseeing vehicle maintenance, driver management, fuel monitoring, regulatory compliance, fleet documentation, and transportation logistics to support uninterrupted business operations.
	Also, ensuring that all vehicles are properly maintained, compliant with statutory requirements, and operated in accordance with company policies, while driving initiatives that improve fleet performance, reduce operational costs, and enhance safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of RFL fleet, including vehicles used for delivery, logistics, and staff transportation.
	Schedule and coordinate vehicle maintenance, repairs, and inspections to ensure optimal performance and minimize downtime.
	Monitor fuel consumption and implement cost-saving measures.
	Maintain accurate records of vehicle usage, mileage, and maintenance history.
	Ensure all vehicles are properly insured, licensed, and compliant with local regulations.
	Coordinate with drivers and logistics teams to ensure timely and efficient delivery of goods.
	Prepare and maintain fleet-related documentation, including contracts, permits, and reports.
	Monitor and manage fleet-related expenses, including fuel, maintenance, and repairs.
	Handle driver-related administrative tasks, such as licensing, training, and performance monitoring
	Prepare and maintain fleet-related documentation, including contracts, permits, and reports.
	Monitor and manage fleet-related expenses, including fuel, maintenance, and repairs.
	Assist in the procurement of new vehicles and disposal of old or non-functional vehicles.


Qualifications


	First Degree or its equivalent in Business Administration or any related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of relevant work experience in a well-structured FMCG or Manufacturing environment.
	Professional certification in Nigerian Institute of Management (Chartered), Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM), etc. is an added advantage


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Fleet Management Skills
	Filing and Documentation Skills
	Vendor/Contractor Management Skills
	Admin-related Expenses Management Skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: talents@ritefoodsltd.com using the Job Positopn as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

We promote a culture of fairness and equal access to opportunities for all qualified applicants.

Fleet Officer Job at Rite Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks’ segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are recruiting to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Locations: Port Harcourt - Rivers, and Benin - Edo

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Fleet Officer is responsible for managing and coordinating the organization's fleet operations to ensure the safe, efficient, and cost-effective utilization of vehicles.
	You will be overseeing vehicle maintenance, driver management, fuel monitoring, regulatory compliance, fleet documentation, and transportation logistics to support uninterrupted business operations.
	Also, ensuring that all vehicles are properly maintained, compliant with statutory requirements, and operated in accordance with company policies, while driving initiatives that improve fleet performance, reduce operational costs, and enhance safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of RFL fleet, including vehicles used for delivery, logistics, and staff transportation.
	Schedule and coordinate vehicle maintenance, repairs, and inspections to ensure optimal performance and minimize downtime.
	Monitor fuel consumption and implement cost-saving measures.
	Maintain accurate records of vehicle usage, mileage, and maintenance history.
	Ensure all vehicles are properly insured, licensed, and compliant with local regulations.
	Coordinate with drivers and logistics teams to ensure timely and efficient delivery of goods.
	Prepare and maintain fleet-related documentation, including contracts, permits, and reports.
	Monitor and manage fleet-related expenses, including fuel, maintenance, and repairs.
	Handle driver-related administrative tasks, such as licensing, training, and performance monitoring
	Prepare and maintain fleet-related documentation, including contracts, permits, and reports.
	Monitor and manage fleet-related expenses, including fuel, maintenance, and repairs.
	Assist in the procurement of new vehicles and disposal of old or non-functional vehicles.


Qualifications


	First Degree or its equivalent in Business Administration or any related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of relevant work experience in a well-structured FMCG or Manufacturing environment.
	Professional certification in Nigerian Institute of Management (Chartered), Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM), etc. is an added advantage


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Fleet Management Skills
	Filing and Documentation Skills
	Vendor/Contractor Management Skills
	Admin-related Expenses Management Skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Positopn as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Equal Opportunity Statement: We are an equal opportunity provider. We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate on the basis of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, disability. We promote a culture of fairness and equal access to opportunities for all qualified applicants.
